title: BUG #18764: server closed the connection unexpectedly
description:
The following bug has been logged on the website:
Bug reference:      18764
Logged by:          Jason Smith
Email address:      dqetool(at)126(dot)com
PostgreSQL version: 17.2
Operating system:   Linux
Description:
Run the following statements and an error is returned.
CREATE TABLE t0 (c1 INT, c2 DECIMAL);
INSERT INTO t0 VALUES (0, NULL);
INSERT INTO t0 VALUES (8, NULL);
SELECT c2 AS ca2, c2 AS ca3 FROM t0 UNION SELECT DISTINCT ca8 AS ca5, ca7 AS
ca6 FROM (SELECT c1 AS ca7, c2 AS ca8 FROM t0) AS ta1 JOIN (SELECT c1 AS
ca10, c1 AS ca11 FROM t0) AS ta2 ON TRUE; -- server closed the connection
unexpectedly
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value INT, amount DECIMAL);
INSERT INTO t1 (value, amount) VALUES (1, NULL);
SELECT amount AS a1, amount AS a2 FROM t1 UNION SELECT DISTINCT b1 AS b3, b2 AS b4 FROM (SELECT id AS b1, amount AS b2 FROM t1) AS sub1 JOIN (SELECT id AS b5, value AS b6 FROM t1) AS sub2 ON TRUE;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t1 WHERE amount IS NULL;
key_bug_features: SELECT with UNION on NULL values causes connection closure
